figures.caption: Figure 4: The heat equation on Ω1 = [−1, 1]; we show the errors Er,p for different values of r, measured with p = ∞ (blue) and p = 2 (red). We also show present the L∞ (yellow) and L2 (purple) norms of the residuals.

Figure 5: The heat equation on the square box Ω2; the errors Er,p are shown for different values of r, measured with p = ∞ (blue) and p = 2 (red), as well as the averaged L∞ (yellow) and L2 (purple) norms of the residuals.

Figure 6: The heat equation in the L-shaped domain Ω3. We present the errors Er,p for different values of r, measured with p = ∞ (blue) and p = 2 (red), along with the averaged L∞ (yellow) and L2 (purple) norms of the residuals.

Figure 7: The steady viscous Burgers’ equation (10) on the 1D interval Ω1. The errors for different values of r are shown, measured in L∞ (blue) and L2 (red), along with the L∞ (yellow) and L2 (purple) norms of the residuals.

Figure 8: The Poisson–Boltzmann equation (11) on the 1D interval Ω1. The errors for different values of r are shown, measured in L∞ (blue) and L2 (red), along with the L∞ (yellow) and L2 (purple) norms of the residuals.

Figure 1: Poisson equation on the one dimensional domain Ω1. The errors ∥ur − u∥ for different values of r, measured in L∞ (blue) and L2 (red), for problem (i), across PINN architectures. We also show the PINN errors ∥uθ NN − u∥ in L∞ (yellow) and L2 (purple) as the horizontal curves. The residuals ∥er∥ in in L∞ (green) and L2 (cyan) are also shown; note the close similarity in the behaviour of the error and residual curves.

Figure 2: Poisson equation in the square box Ω2. The errors ∥ur − u∥ for different values of r, measured in L∞ (blue) and L2 (red), for problem (ii), across PINN architectures. We also show the PINN errors ∥uθ NN − u∥ in L∞ (yellow) and L2 (purple) as the horizontal curves. The residuals ∥er∥ in in L∞ (green) and L2 (cyan) are also shown; note the close similarity in the behaviour of the error and residual curves.

Figure 3: Poisson equation on the L-shaped domain Ω3. The errors ∥ur − u∥ for different values of r, measured in L∞ (blue) and L2 (red), for problem (iii), across PINN architectures. We also show the PINN errors ∥uθ NN − u∥ in L∞ (yellow) and L2 (purple) as the horizontal curves. The residuals ∥er∥ in in L∞ (green) and L2 (cyan) are also shown; note the close similarity in the behaviour of the error and residual curves.
